query: Did Rosatom evacuate staff from Bushehr and put reactor in safe shutdown July 2026? Status of Bushehr reactor amid Iran war strikes early-mid July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the key summary:

**Did Rosatom evacuate staff and place the reactor in safe shutdown in July 2026?** The answer requires disambiguation:

- **The evacuation happened in EARLY APRIL 2026, not July.** Rosatom evacuated 200+ staff (of ~320 Russians) to Armenia amid US-Israeli strikes, completing it around April 20, 2026, leaving ~24 "volunteers" to maintain the reactor's safe operability.

- **By early-mid July 2026, the trend REVERSED.** On July 10, 2026, Rosatom began *returning* staff (first group of six) after Russia-IAEA talks in Kaliningrad; Interfax reported staff would resume returning "from mid-July."

- **On "safe shutdown":** The reactor was never directly hit, and IAEA confirmed no radiation increase. However, I could **not** find explicit confirmation of the exact term "safe shutdown"/"cold shutdown" being used — the documented posture was a volunteer skeleton crew "maintaining operability" of the units. This is an important caveat flagged in my findings.

So if the forecasting question hinges on a July 2026 evacuation specifically, the factual record points to **return/redeployment in July**, with the actual evacuation and protective posture dating to **April 2026**.

sources.title: Rosatom Prepares Final Staff Evacuation From Bushehr Nuclear Plant
sources.snippet: Rosatom will carry out a final evacuation of employees; more than 200 people expected to leave for Armenia, while 'volunteers' remain to keep facility operational; ~320 Russians in area.

sources.title: Rosatom has completed the evacuation of specialists from Bushehr
sources.snippet: Rosatom completed evacuation; two dozen volunteers remain to maintain operability of nuclear power units despite risks, Likhachev said.

sources.title: Russian staff to return to Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant from mid July, Ifax cites Rosatom
sources.snippet: Interfax cites Rosatom that Russian staff will return to Bushehr from mid-July 2026.

sources.title: Russia's Rosatom Begins Returning Staff to Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
sources.snippet: July 10, 2026: Likhachev says first group of six personnel began traveling back to Bushehr after Russia-IAEA talks in Kaliningrad; decision pending on more staff; plant not targeted.

sources.title: Rosatom to Resume Work at Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Plant After Wartime Evacuation
sources.snippet: Rosatom intends to redeploy engineers and technical staff to Bushehr to resume construction work after wartime evacuation.

sources.title: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
sources.snippet: Iran claimed the area near the plant was attacked four times during the 2026 Iran war as of 5 April 2026, killing at least one plant employee, while the IAEA reported no radiation level rise.

sources.title: The Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
sources.snippet: Projectile struck within 350m of active reactor March 17, 2026; second strike March 24; third confirmed by IAEA March 28; Iran/Rosatom confirmed no reactor damage.

sources.title: UN nuclear agency chief 'deeply concerned' by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant
sources.snippet: IAEA chief Grossi deeply concerned over fourth projectile strike near Bushehr.

sources.title: Russian State Atomic Energy Corporation head says 639 of its employees remain in Iran
sources.snippet: Rosatom head states 639 of its employees remain in Iran.

sources.title: Russia prepares final evacuation of staff from Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant
sources.snippet: Likhachev at St. Petersburg briefing: final wave of evacuation, more than 200 people, expected next week; described as priority.
